target.name: Spaceium
target.founder: Ashi Dissanayake (CEO, University of Ottawa; orbital mechanics + propulsion; NASA aerodynamics + asteroid return-mission simulations), Reza Fetanat (CTO, University of Ottawa; mechanical, cryogenics; revitalized the LR-101 engine used on Atlas/Thor/Delta)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 36.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Spaceium is a Seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Ashi Dissanayake (CEO; orbital mechanics and propulsion expertise from NASA) and Reza Fetanat (CTO; cryogenics and legacy engine experience). The company has raised $6.3M to date. The relevant comp universe spans 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though no direct same-stage comparable exists with sufficient data density.

Of three valuation methods tested, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. Using total capital raised ($6.3M) as a proxy for Seed-stage value and applying a 2.5x multiplier to next-stage progression yields $15.8M. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue (standard for private companies). Peer-Median analysis failed the minimum threshold of three comparable datapoints within narrow stage and tier filters. The public Space & Aerospace median trades at 36.9x revenue, but this cannot be operationalized without target revenue.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $18M, with $16M as the central estimate. This range reflects reliance on a single method; key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier for this specific subsector, and the lack of validated peer multiples at Seed stage in aerospace. Revenue trajectory and technical de-risking milestones will be critical to validation at next funding round.
